organizations.name: President of the Republic of Singapore
organizations.subcategory: Head of State / 国家元首
organizations.description: The President is the Head of State of Singapore. Elected by citizens, the President exercises custodial powers over reserves and key appointments.

organizations.name: The Cabinet
organizations.subcategory: Executive Branch / 行政机关（内阁）
organizations.description: The Cabinet forms the executive branch together with the President. Led by the Prime Minister, it is responsible for all government policies and day-to-day administration of state affairs.

organizations.name: Prime Minister's Office
organizations.description: The PMO comprises agencies and statutory boards that support the Prime Minister. Includes MAS, Civil Service College, and key strategy units.

organizations.name: Ministry of Finance
organizations.description: Oversees fiscal policy, government budgeting, and financial regulation. Sole shareholder of Temasek Holdings.

organizations.name: Ministry of Defence
organizations.description: Responsible for defence policy and overseeing the Singapore Armed Forces.

organizations.name: Ministry of Foreign Affairs
organizations.description: Responsible for managing Singapore's diplomatic relations and foreign policy.

organizations.name: Ministry of Home Affairs
organizations.description: Responsible for internal security, law enforcement, immigration, and civil defence. Oversees SPF, SCDF, ICA, ISD, CNB.

organizations.name: Ministry of Education
organizations.description: Responsible for education policy, managing schools, and overseeing higher education institutions.

organizations.name: Ministry of Health
organizations.description: Responsible for healthcare policy, public health, and regulation of medical services.

organizations.name: Ministry of Trade and Industry
organizations.description: Responsible for trade policy, economic development, and industry promotion. Oversees EDB, A*STAR, ESG, JTC, STB.

organizations.name: Ministry of Transport
organizations.description: Responsible for transport policy and infrastructure. Oversees LTA, CAAS, MPA.

organizations.name: Ministry of National Development
organizations.description: Responsible for land use planning, public housing, and urban development. Oversees HDB, URA, NParks, BCA, SLA.

organizations.name: Ministry of Manpower
organizations.description: Responsible for labour policy, workforce development, and employment regulation. Oversees CPF Board, WSG.

organizations.name: Ministry of Law
organizations.description: Responsible for legal policy, intellectual property, and land registration. Oversees IPOS, SLA.

organizations.name: Ministry of Culture, Community and Youth
organizations.description: Responsible for arts, heritage, community engagement, and youth development. Oversees NAC, NHB, NLB, PA, SportSG.

organizations.name: Ministry of Digital Development and Information
organizations.description: Responsible for digital policy, info-communications, media regulation, and government communications. Oversees IMDA, GovTech, NLB.

organizations.name: Ministry of Social and Family Development
organizations.description: Responsible for social welfare, family policy, and child protection. Oversees NCSS.

organizations.name: Ministry of Sustainability and the Environment
organizations.description: Responsible for environmental sustainability, water resources, and food safety. Oversees NEA, PUB, SFA, NParks.

organizations.name: Parliament of Singapore
organizations.description: Unicameral legislature of Singapore. Makes the laws of the land. Comprises elected, non-constituency, and nominated Members of Parliament.

organizations.name: Supreme Court of Singapore
organizations.subcategory: Judiciary / 司法机关 - 最高法院
organizations.description: Highest court in Singapore. Comprises the Court of Appeal and the High Court. Hears civil and criminal cases as well as appeals from lower courts.

organizations.name: State Courts of Singapore
organizations.subcategory: Judiciary / 司法机关 - 国家法院
organizations.description: Subordinate courts handling civil and criminal cases below the High Court threshold. Includes District Courts, Magistrates' Courts, and specialist courts.

organizations.name: Family Justice Courts
organizations.subcategory: Judiciary / 司法机关 - 家事司法法院
organizations.description: Specialist courts handling family-related proceedings including divorce, custody, and adoption.

organizations.name: Attorney-General's Chambers
organizations.subcategory: Independent Organ of State / 独立国家机关
organizations.description: Provides legal advice to the government, drafts legislation, and prosecutes criminal cases. Headed by the Attorney-General.

organizations.name: Auditor-General's Office
organizations.subcategory: Independent Organ of State / 独立国家机关
organizations.description: Independent audit body that audits government ministries, departments, and statutory boards to ensure accountability.

organizations.name: Public Service Commission
organizations.subcategory: Independent Organ of State / 独立国家机关
organizations.description: Constitutional body responsible for appointment, promotion, and discipline of public officers in Singapore.

organizations.name: Industrial Arbitration Court
organizations.subcategory: Independent Organ of State / 独立国家机关
organizations.description: Specialised court that adjudicates trade disputes between employers and employees/trade unions.

organizations.name: Monetary Authority of Singapore
organizations.description: Central bank and financial regulatory authority of Singapore, responsible for monetary policy, financial stability, and supervision of financial institutions.

organizations.name: Economic Development Board
organizations.description: Statutory board under the Ministry of Trade and Industry responsible for planning and executing economic strategies to enhance Singapore's position as a global business centre.

organizations.name: Accounting and Corporate Regulatory Authority
aliases: 新加坡会计与企业管制局
organizations.description: Statutory board under the Ministry of Finance that regulates business registration, financial reporting, and public accountants in Singapore.

organizations.name: Inland Revenue Authority of Singapore
organizations.description: Statutory board under the Ministry of Finance responsible for collecting taxes and administering tax laws.

organizations.name: Housing and Development Board
organizations.description: Statutory board under the Ministry of National Development responsible for public housing in Singapore.

organizations.name: Central Provident Fund Board
organizations.description: Statutory board under the Ministry of Manpower that manages Singapore's compulsory savings plan for retirement, healthcare, and housing.

organizations.name: Infocomm Media Development Authority
organizations.description: Statutory board under the Ministry of Communications and Information that regulates the info-communications and media sectors.

organizations.name: Land Transport Authority
organizations.description: Statutory board under the Ministry of Transport responsible for planning, building, and maintaining land transport infrastructure.

organizations.name: National Environment Agency
organizations.description: Statutory board under the Ministry of Sustainability and the Environment responsible for environmental protection and public health.
organizations.parent_organization: Ministry of Sustainability and the Environment (MSE)

organizations.name: Government Technology Agency
organizations.description: Statutory board under the Prime Minister's Office that drives Singapore's Smart Nation initiative and delivers digital government services.

organizations.name: Temasek Holdings
organizations.description: State-owned investment company wholly owned by the Minister for Finance (Singapore). Manages a portfolio of S$382 billion (2024) across various sectors globally.

organizations.name: Singapore Telecommunications
organizations.description: Largest telecommunications company in Singapore, majority-owned by Temasek Holdings. Listed on SGX.

organizations.name: Singapore Airlines
organizations.description: Flag carrier airline of Singapore, majority-owned by Temasek Holdings. One of the world's leading airlines.

organizations.name: PSA International
organizations.description: One of the world's largest port operators, wholly owned by Temasek Holdings. Operates port facilities across Asia, Europe, and the Americas.

organizations.name: GIC Private Limited
organizations.description: Sovereign wealth fund established by the Government of Singapore to manage Singapore's foreign reserves. One of the world's largest SWFs with estimated assets exceeding US$770 billion.

organizations.name: SMRT Corporation
organizations.description: Multi-modal public transport operator in Singapore, wholly owned by Temasek Holdings. Operates MRT, bus, and taxi services.

organizations.name: Mediacorp
organizations.description: State-owned media conglomerate of Singapore, owned by Temasek Holdings. Largest content creator and broadcaster operating TV, radio, and digital media.

organizations.name: CapitaLand
organizations.description: One of Asia's largest real estate companies, majority-owned by Temasek Holdings.

organizations.name: Surbana Jurong
organizations.description: Government-owned engineering and infrastructure consulting firm, majority-owned by Temasek Holdings.

organizations.name: Singapore Post
organizations.description: Singapore's designated postal service provider. Originally a statutory board, corporatised in 1992.

organizations.name: Sea Limited
organizations.description: Global technology company founded in Singapore, operating Garena (gaming), Shopee (e-commerce), and Monee (fintech). Largest company in Southeast Asia by market cap.

organizations.name: Grab Holdings
organizations.description: Southeast Asia's leading superapp for ride-hailing, food delivery, and digital payments. Founded in Malaysia, headquartered in Singapore.

organizations.name: Wilmar International
organizations.description: Asia's leading agribusiness group and one of the largest listed palm oil companies by market capitalisation.

organizations.name: Olam Group
organizations.description: Major food and agri-business company operating in 60 countries, supplying ingredients and raw materials.

organizations.name: DBS Bank
organizations.description: Largest bank in Southeast Asia by assets. Largest bank in Singapore with assets of USD 509.1 billion.

organizations.name: Oversea-Chinese Banking Corporation
organizations.description: Second-largest financial services group in Southeast Asia by assets. Assets of approximately USD 402.2 billion.

organizations.name: United Overseas Bank
organizations.description: Third-largest bank in Singapore and a leading bank in Southeast Asia. Assets of approximately USD 340.7 billion.

organizations.name: National University of Singapore
organizations.description: Flagship national research university of Singapore. Consistently ranked among the top universities in Asia and the world.

organizations.name: Nanyang Technological University
organizations.description: Major research-intensive university in Singapore. Ranked among the world's top universities.

organizations.name: Singapore Management University
organizations.description: Autonomous university specializing in business and management studies.

organizations.name: Singapore University of Technology and Design
organizations.description: Fourth autonomous university of Singapore, established in collaboration with MIT.

organizations.name: Singapore Institute of Technology
organizations.description: Fifth autonomous university of Singapore, focused on applied learning.

organizations.name: Agency for Science, Technology and Research
organizations.description: Statutory board under the Ministry of Trade and Industry, leading Singapore's public sector research and development.

organizations.name: SPH Media
organizations.description: Singapore's largest omnichannel media network. Publishes The Straits Times, Lianhe Zaobao, and other major newspapers.

organizations.name: Channel NewsAsia
organizations.description: English-language news channel owned by Mediacorp. Recognised as Channel of the Year by the Association for International Broadcasting.

organizations.name: The Straits Times
organizations.description: English-language daily newspaper published by SPH Media. Singapore's most widely-read English newspaper.

organizations.name: Lianhe Zaobao
organizations.description: Major Chinese-language daily newspaper in Singapore published by SPH Media.

organizations.name: Community Foundation of Singapore
organizations.description: Singapore's oldest and leading community foundation, enabling donors to establish named funds for charitable giving.

organizations.name: Mercy Relief
organizations.description: Leading disaster relief NGO headquartered in Singapore, providing emergency aid within 72 hours of disasters in the Asia-Pacific region.

organizations.name: Association of Women for Action and Research
organizations.description: Singapore's leading gender equality advocacy organisation.

organizations.name: National Volunteer and Philanthropy Centre
organizations.description: Non-profit, non-government national organisation that promotes volunteerism and philanthropy in Singapore.

organizations.name: Singapore International Foundation
organizations.description: Leading nonprofit connecting global communities through volunteer programs, social innovation, and partnerships.

organizations.name: Asia-Pacific Economic Cooperation Secretariat
organizations.description: Administrative headquarters of APEC, based in Singapore. Provides coordination, technical and advisory support for the 21-member economic cooperation forum.

organizations.name: People's Action Party
organizations.description: Dominant ruling political party of Singapore since 1959. Ideologies include civic nationalism, economic liberalism, pragmatism.

organizations.name: Workers' Party of Singapore
organizations.description: Largest opposition party in Singapore. Ideologies include social democracy, democratic socialism, civic nationalism.

organizations.name: Progress Singapore Party
organizations.description: Political party founded in 2019, currently one of the opposition parties in Parliament.

organizations.name: Singapore Democratic Party
organizations.description: Political party advocating social liberal policies.

organizations.name: Red Dot United
organizations.description: Political party founded in 2020.

organizations.name: Ministry of Defence
organizations.description: Ministry responsible for Singapore's defence policy and overseeing the Singapore Armed Forces.

organizations.name: Singapore Armed Forces
organizations.description: Military of Singapore with four service branches: Army, Navy, Air Force, and Digital and Intelligence Service (DIS).

organizations.name: Digital and Intelligence Service
organizations.description: Fourth service branch of the SAF established in 2022, consolidating C4I and cyber capabilities. Includes Defence Cyber Command (DCCOM) and SAF C4 and Digitalisation Command (SAFC4DC).

organizations.name: Internal Security Department
organizations.description: Domestic intelligence agency responsible for countering threats to Singapore's internal security, including espionage, subversion, and terrorism.

organizations.name: Military Security Department
organizations.description: Formed in 1975 to counter espionage, subversion and sabotage against MINDEF/SAF.

organizations.name: Defence Science and Technology Agency
organizations.description: Statutory board under MINDEF responsible for driving defence technology development and systems acquisition.

statutory_boards_full_list: Agency for Science, Technology and Research (A*STAR)
Competition and Consumer Commission of Singapore (CCCS)
Home Team Science and Technology Agency (HTX)
Maritime and Port Authority of Singapore (MPA)

GOV.description: 包括：国家元首+内阁(2)、16个部委、国会(1)、法院体系(3)、独立国家机关(4)、66个法定机构+附属机构

SOE.description: Government-linked companies via Temasek Holdings and GIC

CORP.description: Major publicly listed and tech companies headquartered in Singapore

ACAD.description: Universities, polytechnics, and research institutes from Wikidata

MEDIA.description: Major media organizations (duopoly: Mediacorp + SPH Media)

FIN.description: Singapore's Big Three banks (also top in Southeast Asia)

PARTY.description: Political parties (active and historical) from Wikidata

data_quality.notes: GOV category includes 66 statutory boards from Wikipedia list. PARTY includes 25 unique parties from Wikidata. Wikidata rate limiting prevented full SPARQL queries for SOE/NGO/INTL/MIL - supplemented via web search.
